assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and '## Action

Sub-task created to address this feedback.' The file subtask-30001.md exists in the outputs directory containing the sub-task description. The report.md also confirms: '30001 | Code change request | Wrap soft_delete UPDATEs in a transaction | Sub-task created'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: suggestion' and the Reasoning section explicitly explains: 'The reviewer uses suggestive language: "should also add" and "would help." The phrase "should also" proposes an addition beyond the current scope rather than identifying a defect in the existing code. The phrase "would help" frames this as a performance improvement that is beneficial but not strictly required for correctness.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and '## Action

No sub-task created. This is a nit -- minor style feedback about error message wording that does not affect correctness.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and '## Action

No sub-task created. This is a question asking for clarification about design intent; no code change is requested.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7: wrapping UPDATE statements), '## Files to Modify' (line 20: 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs'), '## Implementation Notes' (line 23: SeaORM transaction details), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 30: four acceptance criteria items).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 13) with the full original comment text: '> The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction. If the sbom_advisory update fails after sbom_package succeeds, you'll have inconsistent state. Wrap the three operations in `self.db.transaction(|txn| { ... })` and use `txn` instead of `self.db` for each exec call.' along with Comment ID (30001), Author (reviewer-a), and File reference.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only markdown files: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. No code files (.rs, .toml, .json, .yaml, etc.) exist in the outputs directory. All output files are classification reports, sub-task descriptions, or the verification report.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md) in the outputs directory. The report.md Review Feedback row references all four comments: '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping), 1 suggestion (comment 30002: index addition), 1 nit (comment 30003: context message), 1 question (comment 30004: GET behavior).' The Review Feedback Summary table lists all four comments with their classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging the PR. The overall status is 'WARN' (not merged). The report concludes with a summary of findings and sub-task creation. There is no merge action, merge command, or merge indication anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (not present on base branch). All test changes are additions; no existing tests were modified or deleted. |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed '## Convention Upgrade Eligibility' section that evaluates three criteria: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check — 'no documented convention requiring indexes on deleted_at columns or nullable filter columns in migrations'; (2) Codebase pattern check — 'insufficient migration history to establish a counted pattern'; (3) Performance-related scrutiny — 'upgrading a suggestion requires either a documented convention in CONVENTIONS.md or a demonstrated codebase pattern with multiple counted occurrences.' The upgrade decision is: 'No upgrade. The suggestion does not match a documented CONVENTIONS.md convention and lacks a demonstrated codebase pattern.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30002.md states '## Action

No sub-task created. This is a suggestion proposing a performance optimization that is not backed by a documented project convention or established codebase pattern.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report.md Review Feedback Summary table confirms: '30002 | Suggestion | Add partial index on deleted_at column | No sub-task (not backed by documented convention or established codebase pattern)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found on the PR.' This confirms the eval quality detection found no matches and is reported as N/A, not affecting the Test Quality combination (which is PASS).
